formulas.name: Peter Drucker's Definition
formulas.statement: Management is a set of processes that can be learned, practiced, and used, and it is not a mystery.

formulas.name: Law of Unity of Command
formulas.statement: An employee should receive instructions from only one superior, and he should be responsible to only one superior.

formulas.name: Law of Unity of Direction
formulas.statement: Each group should have one head who is responsible for the group and its activities.
formulas.notes: Need for a single authority figure

formulas.name: Law of Subordination of Individual Interest to the Interest of the Organization
formulas.statement: The interest of the organization should take precedence over the individual interest of its members.
formulas.notes: Importance of organizational goals over personal interests

formulas.name: Law of Span of Control
formulas.statement: A manager can manage a certain number of subordinates, known as the span of control, and beyond which it becomes unmanageable.
formulas.notes: Optimal number of subordinates for effective management

formulas.name: Fayol's 14 Principles of Management
formulas.statement: Planning, Organizing, Commanding, Coordinating, Controlling, etc. are the 14 principles of management by Henri Fayol.

formulas.name: Management Functions
formulas.statement: Planning, Organizing, Staffing, Directing, Controlling, etc. are the main functions of management.
